nodes.name: White-rumped monjita
nodes.summary: White-rumped monjita（White-rumped monjita，学名 Xolmis velatus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联10个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括稀树草原。

nodes.name: 南马托格罗索州
nodes.summary: 南马托格罗索州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联43种鸟类，包括孤䳍、Speckled chachalaca、蓝眼地鸠等43种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The white-rumped monjita (Xolmis velatus) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay, and as a vagrant to Argentina.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The white-rumped monjita is found in Brazil from the lower Amazon River in Pará and Maranhão south to Paraná and occasionally beyond, and west to southern Mato Grosso and Mato Grosso do Sul

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: | last2=Rumboll |first2=Maurice| title =Birds of Southern South America and Antarctica | publisher =Princeton University Press | series =Princeton Illustrated Checklists | date =2001 | location =New Jersey | pages =Plate 75, map 75.4 | isbn =0-691-09035-1 }} The SACC also has records of it as a vagrant in northern Argentina. The white-rumped monjita inhabits open and semi-open landscapes such as savanna and grasslands with some bushes and trees, and is usually found near water
